title: rubus serpens subsp. incultus
intro: Rubus serpens subsp. incultus, also known as the wild raspberry or bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus serpens species, a type of wild raspberry plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus serpens subsp. incultus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
